1	"""Upload provenance schema (M-11 — Phase B).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md: "Page/sheet/slide/timecode provenance map (NOT
4	just char offsets)". When M-12's Question-Bound Corpus Brief
5	emits a cited paragraph, the citation must trace back to a precise
6	location in the source upload — page X of a PDF, cell A3 of a
7	spreadsheet, slide 12, or seconds 38.5–42.1 of an audio file.
8	
9	Char offsets alone are not enough: a multi-page PDF flattened to
10	text loses the page break information; a spreadsheet flattened to
11	CSV loses the cell coordinates; a presentation flattened to text
12	loses the slide structure.
13	
14	This module defines a TAGGED UNION of provenance variants. Every
15	variant carries `upload_id` so the chunk traces back to a row in
16	the `uploads` table. Specific location fields differ per kind.
17	
18	Phase B emits only `TextSpan` and `PdfSpan` (TextParser is
19	shipped; PdfParser is a stub). The schema accepts all variants
20	from day 1 so Phase C parsers (sheet / slide / audio) plug in
21	without a schema migration.
22	
23	Distinct from `audit_ir.loader.EvidenceSpanToken` which models
24	V30's `[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]` per-sentence evidence bindings —
25	that's report-side; this is upload-side.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
31	from typing import Any, ClassVar
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class TextSpan:
36	    """Plain-text upload — char offsets within the upload's
37	    extracted text. The fallback when no richer location info is
38	    available."""
39	
40	    upload_id: str
41	    char_start: int
42	    char_end: int
43	
44	    kind: ClassVar[str] = "text_span"
45	
46	
47	@dataclass(frozen=True)
48	class PdfSpan:
49	    """A region within a single page of a PDF. char offsets are
50	    relative to the PAGE's extracted text (so page 5's offsets
51	    don't bleed into page 6)."""
52	
53	    upload_id: str
54	    page: int  # 1-indexed
55	    char_start: int
56	    char_end: int
57	
58	    kind: ClassVar[str] = "pdf_span"
59	
60	
61	@dataclass(frozen=True)
62	class SheetCell:
63	    """A cell or cell range within a single sheet of a workbook.
64	
65	    `cell_range` follows the canonical A1-style notation from the
66	    source format ("A1", "B5:D10", "Sheet1!A1") — preserved
67	    verbatim so the citation matches what users see in Excel /
68	    Google Sheets.
69	    """
70	
71	    upload_id: str
72	    sheet: str
73	    cell_range: str
74	
75	    kind: ClassVar[str] = "sheet_cell"
76	
77	
78	@dataclass(frozen=True)
79	class SlideRegion:
80	    """A region within a single slide of a presentation.
81	
82	    `bbox` is optional (x_left, y_top, width, height) in slide
83	    coordinates. None means "the whole slide".
84	    """
85	
86	    upload_id: str
87	    slide_num: int  # 1-indexed
88	    bbox: tuple[float, float, float, float] | None = None
89	
90	    kind: ClassVar[str] = "slide_region"
91	
92	
93	@dataclass(frozen=True)
94	class Timecode:
95	    """A time range within an audio or video upload, in seconds."""
96	
97	    upload_id: str
98	    start_s: float
99	    end_s: float
100	
101	    kind: ClassVar[str] = "timecode"
102	
103	
104	# Tagged union — every variant carries `kind` for serialization.
105	UploadProvenance = TextSpan | PdfSpan | SheetCell | SlideRegion | Timecode
106	
107	
108	_KIND_TO_CLASS: dict[str, type] = {
109	    TextSpan.kind: TextSpan,
110	    PdfSpan.kind: PdfSpan,
111	    SheetCell.kind: SheetCell,
112	    SlideRegion.kind: SlideRegion,
113	    Timecode.kind: Timecode,
114	}
115	
116	
117	def to_dict(prov: UploadProvenance) -> dict[str, Any]:
118	    """Serialize a provenance variant to a dict with `kind` tag.
119	    Round-trips with `from_dict`."""
120	    out = asdict(prov)
121	    out["kind"] = prov.kind
122	    return out
123	
124	
125	def from_dict(data: dict[str, Any]) -> UploadProvenance:
126	    """Deserialize a dict (as produced by `to_dict`) back into the
127	    correct variant. Raises ValueError on unknown / malformed
128	    `kind`.
129	
130	    Codex M-11 review fix: validates field types in addition to
131	    arity. Without this, malformed payloads with wrong types
132	    (e.g. `SlideRegion(bbox=[1, 2])` — wrong tuple length, or
133	    string offsets where ints are expected) deserialized
134	    successfully via duck typing.
135	    """
136	    kind = data.get("kind")
137	    if kind is None:
138	        raise ValueError("provenance dict missing 'kind' tag")
139	    cls = _KIND_TO_CLASS.get(kind)
140	    if cls is None:
141	        raise ValueError(f"unknown provenance kind: {kind!r}")
142	    fields = {k: v for k, v in data.items() if k != "kind"}
143	    # tuple-typed fields lose type after JSON round-trip — restore.
144	    if cls is SlideRegion and isinstance(fields.get("bbox"), list):
145	        fields["bbox"] = tuple(fields["bbox"])
146	    try:
147	        instance = cls(**fields)
148	    except TypeError as exc:
149	        raise ValueError(f"malformed {kind} provenance: {exc}") from exc
150	    _validate(instance)
151	    return instance
152	
153	
154	def _is_strict_int(v: Any) -> bool:
155	    """Codex M-11 v2 review fix: bool is a subclass of int in
156	    Python, so isinstance(True, int) is True. Validation that
157	    accepts ints must reject booleans explicitly so payloads like
158	    `{"page": true}` don't deserialize as page 1."""
159	    return isinstance(v, int) and not isinstance(v, bool)
160	
161	
162	def _is_strict_number(v: Any) -> bool:
163	    """Same as _is_strict_int but accepts float too. Excludes bool."""
164	    return isinstance(v, (int, float)) and not isinstance(v, bool)
165	
166	
167	def _validate(prov: UploadProvenance) -> None:
168	    """Type and shape validation for a freshly-constructed
169	    provenance variant. Raises ValueError on any issue.
170	
171	    Per LAW II — fail loud, never silently store malformed data.
172	    """
173	    if not isinstance(prov.upload_id, str) or not prov.upload_id:
174	        raise ValueError(
175	            f"malformed {prov.kind}: upload_id must be non-empty string"
176	        )
177	    if isinstance(prov, TextSpan):
178	        if not _is_strict_int(prov.char_start) or not _is_strict_int(prov.char_end):
179	            raise ValueError(
180	                "malformed text_span: char_start/char_end must be int"
181	            )
182	        if prov.char_start < 0 or prov.char_end < prov.char_start:
183	            raise ValueError(
184	                f"malformed text_span: invalid range [{prov.char_start}, {prov.char_end}]"
185	            )
186	    elif isinstance(prov, PdfSpan):
187	        if not _is_strict_int(prov.page) or prov.page < 1:
188	            raise ValueError("malformed pdf_span: page must be int >= 1")
189	        if not _is_strict_int(prov.char_start) or not _is_strict_int(prov.char_end):
190	            raise ValueError(
191	                "malformed pdf_span: char_start/char_end must be int"
192	            )
193	        if prov.char_start < 0 or prov.char_end < prov.char_start:
194	            raise ValueError(
195	                f"malformed pdf_span: invalid range "
196	                f"[{prov.char_start}, {prov.char_end}]"
197	            )
198	    elif isinstance(prov, SheetCell):
199	        if not isinstance(prov.sheet, str) or not prov.sheet:
200	            raise ValueError("malformed sheet_cell: sheet must be non-empty string")
201	        if not isinstance(prov.cell_range, str) or not prov.cell_range:
202	            raise ValueError(
203	                "malformed sheet_cell: cell_range must be non-empty string"
204	            )
205	    elif isinstance(prov, SlideRegion):
206	        if not _is_strict_int(prov.slide_num) or prov.slide_num < 1:
207	            raise ValueError("malformed slide_region: slide_num must be int >= 1")
208	        if prov.bbox is not None:
209	            if not isinstance(prov.bbox, tuple) or len(prov.bbox) != 4:
210	                raise ValueError(
211	                    "malformed slide_region: bbox must be 4-tuple or None"
212	                )
213	            if not all(_is_strict_number(v) for v in prov.bbox):
214	                raise ValueError(
215	                    "malformed slide_region: bbox values must be numeric"
216	                )
217	    elif isinstance(prov, Timecode):
218	        if not _is_strict_number(prov.start_s) or not _is_strict_number(prov.end_s):
219	            raise ValueError("malformed timecode: start_s/end_s must be numeric")
220	        if prov.start_s < 0 or prov.end_s < prov.start_s:
221	            raise ValueError(
222	                f"malformed timecode: invalid range [{prov.start_s}, {prov.end_s}]"
223	            )
224

1	"""Discover available V30 Phase-2 audit artifacts.
2	
3	Phase A scope discipline (per Codex M-2 review): the registry exposes
4	ONLY a curated allowlist of canonical artifacts that successfully load
5	through the strict AuditIR loader. The previous broad `outputs/**/manifest.json`
6	scan picked up 90 directories with 9 slug collisions and 75 of them
7	failed `load_audit_ir()`'s strict schema. Phase A returns exactly the
8	curated set; Phase B/C will replace this with a per-workspace database-
9	backed registry.
10	
11	Each registered run is identified by both:
12	  - `run_id`: the canonical unique identifier (used for routing)
13	  - `slug`: a friendly slug derived from artifact_dir (URL-safe label)
14	
15	`run_id` is the unique key. `slug` may collide if Phase B operators
16	load multiple runs with the same template; the registry detects this
17	at startup and raises.
18	"""
19	
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import json
23	from dataclasses import dataclass
24	from pathlib import Path
25	
26	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
27	OUTPUTS_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "outputs"
28	
29	# Canonical Phase A demo: V30 Phase-2 run-14 (PHASE2_CHECKPOINT ship).
30	CANONICAL_DEMO_SLUG = "clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm"
31	CANONICAL_DEMO_DIR = (
32	    OUTPUTS_DIR
33	    / "full_scale_v30_phase2_run14"
34	    / "clinical"
35	    / "clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm"
36	)
37	
38	# Phase A curated allowlist. Each entry is an absolute artifact directory.
39	# Phase B replaces this with a database-backed per-workspace registry.
40	_PHASE_A_ALLOWLIST: tuple[Path, ...] = (CANONICAL_DEMO_DIR,)
41	
42	
43	@dataclass(frozen=True)
44	class RunSummary:
45	    """Lightweight summary used by the run-list view (no full IR load)."""
46	
47	    slug: str
48	    run_id: str
49	    domain: str
50	    status: str
51	    artifact_dir: Path
52	    cost_usd: float
53	    word_count: int
54	    contradictions_found: int
55	    release_allowed: bool
56	    created_at_iso: str | None
57	
58	
59	class RegistryError(RuntimeError):
60	    """Raised when the curated allowlist contains a malformed or duplicate run."""
61	
62	
63	def _load_run_summary(artifact_dir: Path) -> RunSummary | None:
64	    """Read just manifest.json + protocol.json for the lightweight summary.
65	
66	    Returns None if the directory cannot be summarized — but for a curated
67	    allowlist this should never happen (and the caller raises).
68	    """
69	    manifest_path = artifact_dir / "manifest.json"
70	    if not manifest_path.exists():
71	        return None
72	    try:
73	        with manifest_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
74	            manifest = json.load(f)
75	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
76	        return None
77	
78	    created_at = None
79	    protocol_path = artifact_dir / "protocol.json"
80	    if protocol_path.exists():
81	        try:
82	            with protocol_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
83	                protocol = json.load(f)
84	            created_at = protocol.get("created_at_iso")
85	        except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
86	            created_at = None
87	
88	    generator = manifest.get("generator", {}) or {}
89	    slug = str(manifest.get("slug") or artifact_dir.name)
90	    domain = artifact_dir.parent.name if artifact_dir.parent != OUTPUTS_DIR else ""
91	    return RunSummary(
92	        slug=slug,
93	        run_id=str(manifest.get("run_id", "")),
94	        domain=domain,
95	        status=str(manifest.get("status", "")),
96	        artifact_dir=artifact_dir,
97	        cost_usd=float(manifest.get("cost_usd", 0.0)),
98	        word_count=int(generator.get("words", 0)),
99	        contradictions_found=int(manifest.get("contradictions_found", 0)),
100	        release_allowed=bool(manifest.get("release_allowed", False)),
101	        created_at_iso=created_at,
102	    )
103	
104	
105	def _validate_loadable(artifact_dir: Path) -> None:
106	    """Verify the artifact loads through the strict AuditIR loader.
107	
108	    Raises RegistryError if loading fails. Done at registry init time
109	    so the inspector router never lists a run it can't actually serve.
110	    Imported lazily to avoid a circular import with loader.py.
111	    """
112	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.loader import (
113	        AuditIRSchemaError,
114	        load_audit_ir,
115	    )
116	    try:
117	        load_audit_ir(artifact_dir)
118	    except (FileNotFoundError, AuditIRSchemaError, NotADirectoryError) as exc:
119	        raise RegistryError(
120	            f"Allowlisted artifact failed strict load: {artifact_dir}: {exc}"
121	        ) from exc
122	
123	
124	def _build_runs() -> tuple[RunSummary, ...]:
125	    """Build the registry by validating each allowlisted artifact."""
126	    summaries: list[RunSummary] = []
127	    seen_slugs: dict[str, str] = {}
128	    seen_run_ids: set[str] = set()
129	
130	    for artifact_dir in _PHASE_A_ALLOWLIST:
131	        if not artifact_dir.is_dir():
132	            raise RegistryError(
133	                f"Allowlisted artifact directory missing: {artifact_dir}"
134	            )
135	        _validate_loadable(artifact_dir)
136	        summary = _load_run_summary(artifact_dir)
137	        if summary is None:
138	            raise RegistryError(
139	                f"Allowlisted artifact failed lightweight summary: {artifact_dir}"
140	            )
141	        if summary.run_id in seen_run_ids:
142	            raise RegistryError(
143	                f"Duplicate run_id in allowlist: {summary.run_id}"
144	            )
145	        if summary.slug in seen_slugs and seen_slugs[summary.slug] != summary.run_id:
146	            raise RegistryError(
147	                f"Duplicate slug across distinct run_ids: {summary.slug} "
148	                f"({seen_slugs[summary.slug]} vs {summary.run_id})"
149	            )
150	        seen_slugs[summary.slug] = summary.run_id
151	        seen_run_ids.add(summary.run_id)
152	        summaries.append(summary)
153	
154	    summaries.sort(key=lambda r: (r.created_at_iso or "", r.slug), reverse=True)
155	    return tuple(summaries)
156	
157	
158	# Built once at module import. If the curated allowlist is malformed, this
159	# raises immediately and the inspector route fails to mount — which is what
160	# we want (fail loud, not silent).
161	_RUNS: tuple[RunSummary, ...] = _build_runs()
162	
163	
164	def list_available_runs() -> list[RunSummary]:
165	    """Return every Phase-A-allowlisted V30 audit artifact (already validated)."""
166	    return list(_RUNS)
167	
168	
169	def find_run_by_slug(slug: str) -> RunSummary | None:
170	    """Find a run by slug. Slugs are guaranteed unique within the registry."""
171	    if not slug:
172	        return None
173	    for run in _RUNS:
174	        if run.slug == slug:
175	            return run
176	    return None
177	
178	
179	def find_run_by_id(run_id: str) -> RunSummary | None:
180	    """Find a run by its canonical unique run_id."""
181	    if not run_id:
182	        return None
183	    for run in _RUNS:
184	        if run.run_id == run_id:
185	            return run
186	    return None
187

1	"""Regression alerts (M-18 — Phase C).
2	
3	Across-run companion to M-17 (within-run citation health). Where
4	M-17 catches integrity defects in a single run, M-18 catches the
5	opposite failure mode: the run is internally consistent but
6	materially worse than the baseline (prior successful run of the
7	same slug). Customer-facing audits must not silently regress.
8	
9	Inputs: two loaded `AuditIR` objects — `ir_a` is the baseline
10	(prior successful run of the same slug), `ir_b` is the new run
11	under inspection. The function reuses M-16's `RunDiff` for delta
12	discovery and adds severity classification on top.
13	
14	LAW VI: every threshold is env-overridable. Defaults are
15	conservative-strict in the false-positive direction (better to
16	surface a borderline regression than miss a real one).
17	
18	LAW II: alerts surface as ERROR-equivalent severities (CRITICAL,
19	HIGH). They do not silently degrade; the operator decides whether
20	a HIGH-severity-flagged run still ships.
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import os
26	from dataclasses import dataclass
27	from enum import Enum
28	from typing import Any
29	
30	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.loader import AuditIR
31	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.run_diff import (
32	    ContradictionDelta,
33	    RunDiff,
34	    diff_runs,
35	)
36	
37	
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	# Enumerations
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	
43	class AlertSeverity(Enum):
44	    """Severity tiers for regression alerts.
45	
46	    CRITICAL: ship-blocker. Examples: gate flipped from pass to
47	    fail; release_allowed flipped to False. Operator MUST review.
48	
49	    HIGH: surface prominently. Examples: verified-sentence count
50	    halved; new high-severity contradiction. Operator should
51	    review before sign-off.
52	
53	    MEDIUM: log + surface in alert pane. Material drop but inside
54	    normal-operations range.
55	
56	    INFO: telemetry only.
57	    """
58	
59	    CRITICAL = "critical"
60	    HIGH = "high"
61	    MEDIUM = "medium"
62	    INFO = "info"
63	
64	
65	class AlertCode(Enum):
66	    """Stable issue codes for regression findings."""
67	
68	    ADEQUACY_REGRESSION = "adequacy_regression"
69	    RELEASE_NOT_ALLOWED = "release_not_allowed"
70	    EVALUATOR_GATE_DOWNGRADE = "evaluator_gate_downgrade"
71	    VERIFIED_DROP = "verified_drop"
72	    CITATION_DROP = "citation_drop"
73	    TIER_DOWNGRADE = "tier_downgrade"
74	    NEW_CONTRADICTION = "new_contradiction"
75	    NEW_HIGH_SEVERITY_CONTRADICTION = "new_high_severity_contradiction"
76	    CONTRADICTION_SEVERITY_ESCALATION = "contradiction_severity_escalation"
77	    COST_SPIKE = "cost_spike"
78	
79	
80	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	# Data classes
82	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	
84	
85	@dataclass(frozen=True)
86	class RegressionAlert:
87	    """One regression finding, severity-tagged.
88	
89	    `a_value` and `b_value` are the baseline and new values for
90	    the metric driving the alert (e.g. citation counts, cost in
91	    USD). Optional — set None for boolean-flip alerts.
92	
93	    `threshold` is the env-overridable threshold the alert is
94	    evaluated against, surfaced for telemetry/audit.
95	    """
96	
97	    severity: AlertSeverity
98	    code: AlertCode
99	    message: str
100	    a_value: float | int | str | bool | None = None
101	    b_value: float | int | str | bool | None = None
102	    threshold: float | str | None = None
103	
104	
105	@dataclass(frozen=True)
106	class RegressionReportSummary:
107	    """Aggregate counts + worst-severity convenience field."""
108	
109	    critical_count: int
110	    high_count: int
111	    medium_count: int
112	    info_count: int
113	    worst_severity: str  # "critical" / "high" / "medium" / "info" / "ok"
114	
115	
116	@dataclass(frozen=True)
117	class RegressionReport:
118	    """Surface for the inspector endpoint + audit-bundle attachment."""
119	
120	    a_run_id: str
121	    b_run_id: str
122	    slug: str
123	    alerts: tuple[RegressionAlert, ...]
124	    summary: RegressionReportSummary
125	
126	
127	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	# Thresholds (LAW VI — env-overridable)
129	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
130	
131	
132	def _float_env(key: str, default: float) -> float:
133	    """Return float env var, falling back to default on parse error
134	    or if unset. Matches the pattern used by run_diff / classifier."""
135	    raw = os.environ.get(key)
136	    if raw is None:
137	        return default
138	    try:
139	        v = float(raw)
140	        if v < 0:
141	            return default
142	        return v
143	    except ValueError:
144	        return default
145	
146	
147	def _verified_drop_threshold() -> float:
148	    """Fraction below which a verified-sentence drop counts as
149	    HIGH (defaults to 0.20 — 20% drop). Above 0.50 escalates to
150	    CRITICAL inside the alert generator."""
151	    return _float_env("PG_REGRESSION_VERIFIED_DROP_PCT", 0.20)
152	
153	
154	def _citation_drop_threshold() -> float:
155	    """Fraction below which a citation/bibliography drop counts as
156	    MEDIUM (defaults to 0.20). Above 0.50 escalates to HIGH."""
157	    return _float_env("PG_REGRESSION_CITATION_DROP_PCT", 0.20)
158	
159	
160	def _tier_downgrade_threshold_pp() -> float:
161	    """Percentage-point T1+T2 fraction drop above which a tier
162	    downgrade alert fires (defaults to 10.0 pp)."""
163	    return _float_env("PG_REGRESSION_T1T2_DROP_PP", 10.0)
164	
165	
166	def _cost_spike_ratio() -> float:
167	    """Ratio of new/baseline cost above which a cost-spike alert
168	    fires (defaults to 1.5 — i.e. 50% increase). Ratios above 3.0
169	    escalate from MEDIUM to HIGH.
170	
171	    Codex M-18 v1 review fix: clamp to >= 1.0. A ratio below 1.0
172	    would let an env override flag a CHEAPER run as a cost spike
173	    (false-positive on improvement), so we floor the user-supplied
174	    value at 1.0 and let the default of 1.5 stand otherwise.
175	    """
176	    raw = _float_env("PG_REGRESSION_COST_SPIKE_RATIO", 1.5)
177	    return max(1.0, raw)
178	
179	
180	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	# Detection helpers
182	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
183

207	def _check_evaluator_gate(

209	) -> list[RegressionAlert]:
210	    """gate_class downgrade (e.g. "pass" → "fail" / "blocked").
211	
212	    Treats any gate_class change where the new class is one of
213	    {"fail", "blocked", "abort"} but the baseline was "pass" as
214	    CRITICAL.

216	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
217	    a_gate = ir_a.manifest.evaluator_gate.gate_class.lower()
218	    b_gate = ir_b.manifest.evaluator_gate.gate_class.lower()
219	    failing = {"fail", "blocked", "abort", "rejected"}
220	    if a_gate == "pass" and b_gate in failing:
221	        alerts.append(
222	            RegressionAlert(
223	                severity=AlertSeverity.CRITICAL,
224	                code=AlertCode.EVALUATOR_GATE_DOWNGRADE,
225	                message=(
226	                    f"evaluator gate downgraded from "
227	                    f"{ir_a.manifest.evaluator_gate.gate_class!r} to "
228	                    f"{ir_b.manifest.evaluator_gate.gate_class!r}; "
229	                    f"new run is gate-blocked"
230	                ),
231	                a_value=ir_a.manifest.evaluator_gate.gate_class,

233	            )
234	        )

238	def _check_adequacy(
239	    ir_a: AuditIR, ir_b: AuditIR,
240	) -> list[RegressionAlert]:
241	    """Adequacy gate transition baseline-passing → new-failing is CRITICAL.
242	
243	    Codex M-18 v1 review fix: real V30 corpus_adequacy_gate emits
244	    `proceed | expand | abort` (see corpus_adequacy_gate.py:29) —
245	    NOT `pass/fail/approved`. v1 used the wrong vocabulary, so a
246	    real `proceed → abort` regression produced no alert. v2 uses
247	    the actual decision space and treats both `expand` (corpus
248	    needs more sources) and `abort` (corpus rejected) as failing
249	    states relative to a baseline of `proceed`.
250	    """
251	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
252	    if ir_a.adequacy is None or ir_b.adequacy is None:
253	        return alerts
254	    a_dec = (ir_a.adequacy.decision or "").lower()
255	    b_dec = (ir_b.adequacy.decision or "").lower()
256	    # Real V30 vocabulary first (per corpus_adequacy_gate.py).
257	    # Legacy aliases retained for older artifact directories that
258	    # may still carry the prior pass/fail naming.
259	    passing = {"proceed", "pass", "approved", "adequate"}
260	    failing = {"abort", "expand", "fail", "rejected", "inadequate"}
261	    if a_dec in passing and b_dec in failing:
262	        alerts.append(
263	            RegressionAlert(
264	                severity=AlertSeverity.CRITICAL,
265	                code=AlertCode.ADEQUACY_REGRESSION,
266	                message=(
267	                    f"corpus adequacy decision regressed from "
268	                    f"{ir_a.adequacy.decision!r} to "
269	                    f"{ir_b.adequacy.decision!r}; the new corpus "
270	                    f"no longer passes the gate the baseline corpus "
271	                    f"passed"
272	                ),
273	                a_value=ir_a.adequacy.decision,
274	                b_value=ir_b.adequacy.decision,
275	            )
276	        )
277	    return alerts

280	def _check_verified_drop(
281	    ir_a: AuditIR, ir_b: AuditIR,
282	) -> list[RegressionAlert]:
283	    """Verified-sentence count drop relative to baseline.
284	
285	    Severity ladder:
286	      drop_frac >= 0.50 → CRITICAL
287	      drop_frac >= threshold (default 0.20) → HIGH
288	      else → no alert
289	    """
290	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
291	    a_n = max(ir_a.verified_report.sentences_verified, 0)
292	    b_n = max(ir_b.verified_report.sentences_verified, 0)
293	    if a_n == 0:
294	        # No baseline to compare against; cannot detect a drop.
295	        return alerts
296	    drop_frac = (a_n - b_n) / a_n if a_n > 0 else 0.0
297	    threshold = _verified_drop_threshold()
298	    if drop_frac >= 0.50:
299	        sev = AlertSeverity.CRITICAL
300	    elif drop_frac >= threshold:
301	        sev = AlertSeverity.HIGH
302	    else:
303	        return alerts
304	    alerts.append(
305	        RegressionAlert(
306	            severity=sev,
307	            code=AlertCode.VERIFIED_DROP,
308	            message=(
309	                f"verified-sentence count dropped from {a_n} "
310	                f"(baseline) to {b_n} ({drop_frac*100:.1f}% drop); "
311	                f"the new run produces materially less audited prose"
312	            ),
313	            a_value=a_n,
314	            b_value=b_n,
315	            threshold=threshold,
316	        )
317	    )
318	    return alerts

361	def _high_quality_fraction(ir: AuditIR) -> float:
362	    """Sum of T1 + T2 fractions in the tier mix.
363	
364	    Real V30 manifests use T1..T7 + UNKNOWN (real keys). Older
365	    manifests may use tier1..tier4. Both are tolerated; missing
366	    keys count as 0.
367	    """
368	    f = ir.tier_mix.fractions
369	    return float(
370	        f.get("T1", 0.0) + f.get("T2", 0.0)
371	        + f.get("tier1", 0.0) + f.get("tier2", 0.0)
372	    )

375	def _check_tier_downgrade(
376	    ir_a: AuditIR, ir_b: AuditIR,
377	) -> list[RegressionAlert]:
378	    """T1+T2 fraction drop relative to baseline.
379	
380	    Sources at T1/T2 are the regulatory-grade evidence (FDA labels,
381	    EMA EPARs, RCT primary publications). Replacing them with T3+
382	    sources (reviews, news, clinic notes) materially lowers audit
383	    grade — even if total citation count stays the same.
384	    """
385	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
386	    a_hi = _high_quality_fraction(ir_a)
387	    b_hi = _high_quality_fraction(ir_b)
388	    drop_pp = (a_hi - b_hi) * 100.0
389	    threshold_pp = _tier_downgrade_threshold_pp()
390	    if drop_pp < threshold_pp:
391	        return alerts
392	    if drop_pp >= 30.0:
393	        sev = AlertSeverity.HIGH
394	    else:
395	        sev = AlertSeverity.MEDIUM
396	    alerts.append(
397	        RegressionAlert(
398	            severity=sev,
399	            code=AlertCode.TIER_DOWNGRADE,
400	            message=(
401	                f"high-quality (T1+T2) source share dropped from "
402	                f"{a_hi*100:.1f}% to {b_hi*100:.1f}% "
403	                f"({drop_pp:.1f}pp drop); the new run leans on "
404	                f"lower-tier evidence"
405	            ),
406	            a_value=round(a_hi, 4),
407	            b_value=round(b_hi, 4),
408	            threshold=threshold_pp,
409	        )
410	    )
411	    return alerts

414	# Codex M-18 v1 review: contradiction severity escalation must
415	# also alert. M-16 keys contradictions by (subject, predicate),
416	# so the same cluster persisting across runs but escalating
417	# severity (low → high, medium → critical) doesn't surface as
418	# an "added" delta. Detect escalations directly by walking both
419	# IR's contradictions and matching on (subject, predicate).
420	_SEVERITY_RANK: dict[str, int] = {
421	    "info": 0, "low": 1, "minor": 1,
422	    "medium": 2, "moderate": 2,
423	    "high": 3, "severe": 3, "major": 3,
424	    "critical": 4,
425	}
426	_HIGH_SEVERITY_KEYWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
427	    "high", "critical", "severe", "major",
428	})
429	
430	
431	def _severity_rank(s: str | None) -> int:
432	    """Coerce a contradiction severity string into a totally-
433	    ordered rank for escalation detection. Unknown strings rank 0
434	    so 'unknown' → 'high' looks like an escalation rather than a
435	    sideways move."""
436	    return _SEVERITY_RANK.get((s or "").strip().lower(), 0)
437	
438	
439	def _check_new_contradictions(
440	    diff: RunDiff,
441	) -> list[RegressionAlert]:
442	    """Surface contradictions present in B but not in A.
443	
444	    A new HIGH or CRITICAL severity contradiction is a HIGH alert.
445	    A new MEDIUM or LOW severity contradiction is a MEDIUM alert.
446	    Removed contradictions (resolved disagreement) do not surface
447	    as regressions — they're improvements.
448	    """
449	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
450	    for delta in diff.contradiction_deltas:
451	        if delta.direction != "added":
452	            continue
453	        sev_lc = (delta.severity or "").lower()
454	        if sev_lc in _HIGH_SEVERITY_KEYWORDS:
455	            alerts.append(
456	                RegressionAlert(
457	                    severity=AlertSeverity.HIGH,
458	                    code=AlertCode.NEW_HIGH_SEVERITY_CONTRADICTION,
459	                    message=(
460	                        f"new {delta.severity!r}-severity contradiction "
461	                        f"surfaced in this run on subject "
462	                        f"{delta.subject!r}, predicate "
463	                        f"{delta.predicate!r}; absent in baseline"
464	                    ),
465	                    a_value=None,
466	                    b_value=delta.severity,
467	                )
468	            )
469	        else:
470	            alerts.append(
471	                RegressionAlert(
472	                    severity=AlertSeverity.MEDIUM,
473	                    code=AlertCode.NEW_CONTRADICTION,
474	                    message=(
475	                        f"new contradiction surfaced on subject "
476	                        f"{delta.subject!r}, predicate "
477	                        f"{delta.predicate!r}; absent in baseline"
478	                    ),
479	                    a_value=None,
480	                    b_value=delta.severity,
481	                )
482	            )
483	    return alerts
484	
485	
486	def _check_contradiction_escalation(
487	    ir_a: AuditIR, ir_b: AuditIR,
488	) -> list[RegressionAlert]:
489	    """Codex M-18 v1 review fix: persistent contradictions whose
490	    severity escalates between runs (e.g. 'low' → 'high' on the
491	    same (subject, predicate) cluster) must alert.
492	
493	    M-16's run_diff keys contradictions by (subject, predicate),
494	    so escalation produces no add/remove delta — both runs have
495	    the same key. Walk both IRs' contradictions and match by
496	    (subject, predicate); if rank_b > rank_a, alert.
497	    """
498	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
499	    a_by_key: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {}
500	    for cluster in ir_a.contradictions:
501	        a_by_key[(cluster.subject, cluster.predicate)] = (
502	            cluster.severity or ""
503	        )
504	    for cluster in ir_b.contradictions:
505	        key = (cluster.subject, cluster.predicate)
506	        if key not in a_by_key:
507	            continue  # added cluster — handled by _check_new_contradictions
508	        a_sev = a_by_key[key]
509	        b_sev = cluster.severity or ""
510	        if _severity_rank(b_sev) <= _severity_rank(a_sev):
511	            continue  # same or lower severity — not an escalation
512	        # An escalation to high/critical/severe is HIGH; otherwise
513	        # MEDIUM. This mirrors the new-contradiction severity ladder.
514	        if (b_sev or "").lower() in _HIGH_SEVERITY_KEYWORDS:
515	            sev = AlertSeverity.HIGH
516	        else:
517	            sev = AlertSeverity.MEDIUM
518	        alerts.append(
519	            RegressionAlert(
520	                severity=sev,
521	                code=AlertCode.CONTRADICTION_SEVERITY_ESCALATION,
522	                message=(
523	                    f"contradiction on subject {cluster.subject!r}, "
524	                    f"predicate {cluster.predicate!r} escalated "
525	                    f"severity from {a_sev!r} (baseline) to "
526	                    f"{b_sev!r}; the same disagreement now matters more"
527	                ),
528	                a_value=a_sev,
529	                b_value=b_sev,
530	            )
531	        )
532	    return alerts
533	
534	
535	def _check_cost_spike(
536	    ir_a: AuditIR, ir_b: AuditIR,
537	) -> list[RegressionAlert]:
538	    """Per-run cost (USD) spike relative to baseline.
539	
540	    Severity ladder:
541	      ratio >= 3.0 → HIGH
542	      ratio >= threshold (default 1.5) → MEDIUM
543	      else → no alert
544	    """
545	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
546	    a_cost = float(ir_a.manifest.cost_usd or 0.0)
547	    b_cost = float(ir_b.manifest.cost_usd or 0.0)
548	    if a_cost <= 0:
549	        # No baseline cost; skip to avoid divide-by-zero noise.
550	        return alerts
551	    ratio = b_cost / a_cost
552	    threshold = _cost_spike_ratio()
553	    if ratio >= 3.0:
554	        sev = AlertSeverity.HIGH
555	    elif ratio >= threshold:
556	        sev = AlertSeverity.MEDIUM
557	    else:
558	        return alerts
559	    alerts.append(
560	        RegressionAlert(
561	            severity=sev,
562	            code=AlertCode.COST_SPIKE,
563	            message=(
564	                f"per-run cost rose from ${a_cost:.4f} (baseline) to "
565	                f"${b_cost:.4f} ({ratio:.2f}x increase); investigate "
566	                f"whether quality improvement justifies the spend"
567	            ),
568	            a_value=round(a_cost, 4),
569	            b_value=round(b_cost, 4),
570	            threshold=threshold,
571	        )
572	    )
573	    return alerts

580	
581	def detect_regressions(
582	    ir_a: AuditIR,
583	    ir_b: AuditIR,
584	    *,
585	    diff: RunDiff | None = None,
586	) -> RegressionReport:
587	    """Compare a new run (`ir_b`) against a baseline (`ir_a`).
588	
589	    Both runs MUST share the same slug — otherwise the comparison
590	    is meaningless and we raise ValueError, mirroring `diff_runs`.
591	
592	    `diff` may be passed in if the caller has already computed it
593	    (e.g. the inspector endpoint computes diff + regression in
594	    sequence and shouldn't pay for diff twice). When None, this
595	    function computes it inline.
596	    """
597	    if ir_a.manifest.slug != ir_b.manifest.slug:
598	        raise ValueError(
599	            f"regression compare requires same slug; got "
600	            f"{ir_a.manifest.slug!r} (a) vs {ir_b.manifest.slug!r} (b)"
601	        )
602	
603	    if diff is None:
604	        diff = diff_runs(ir_a, ir_b)
605	    else:
606	        # Codex M-18 v1 review fix: validate caller-supplied diff
607	        # matches the IRs they passed. A mismatched diff would
608	        # inject false contradiction alerts (or hide real ones)
609	        # without any signal that the wires were crossed.
610	        if diff.slug != ir_a.manifest.slug:
611	            raise ValueError(
612	                f"caller-supplied diff has slug {diff.slug!r}; "
613	                f"IRs have slug {ir_a.manifest.slug!r} — refusing "
614	                f"to use a mismatched diff"
615	            )
616	        if diff.a_run_id != ir_a.manifest.run_id:
617	            raise ValueError(
618	                f"caller-supplied diff a_run_id "
619	                f"{diff.a_run_id!r} does not match ir_a.run_id "
620	                f"{ir_a.manifest.run_id!r}"
621	            )
622	        if diff.b_run_id != ir_b.manifest.run_id:
623	            raise ValueError(
624	                f"caller-supplied diff b_run_id "
625	                f"{diff.b_run_id!r} does not match ir_b.run_id "
626	                f"{ir_b.manifest.run_id!r}"
627	            )
628	
629	    alerts: list[RegressionAlert] = []
630	    alerts.extend(_check_release_flip(ir_a, ir_b))
631	    alerts.extend(_check_evaluator_gate(ir_a, ir_b))
632	    alerts.extend(_check_adequacy(ir_a, ir_b))
633	    alerts.extend(_check_verified_drop(ir_a, ir_b))
634	    alerts.extend(_check_citation_drop(ir_a, ir_b))
635	    alerts.extend(_check_tier_downgrade(ir_a, ir_b))
636	    alerts.extend(_check_new_contradictions(diff))
637	    alerts.extend(_check_contradiction_escalation(ir_a, ir_b))
638	    alerts.extend(_check_cost_spike(ir_a, ir_b))
639	
640	    crit = sum(1 for a in alerts if a.severity == AlertSeverity.CRITICAL)
641	    high = sum(1 for a in alerts if a.severity == AlertSeverity.HIGH)
642	    med = sum(1 for a in alerts if a.severity == AlertSeverity.MEDIUM)
643	    info = sum(1 for a in alerts if a.severity == AlertSeverity.INFO)
644	
645	    if crit:
646	        worst = "critical"
647	    elif high:
648	        worst = "high"
649	    elif med:
650	        worst = "medium"
651	    elif info:
652	        worst = "info"
653	    else:
654	        worst = "ok"
655	
656	    summary = RegressionReportSummary(
657	        critical_count=crit,
658	        high_count=high,
659	        medium_count=med,
660	        info_count=info,
661	        worst_severity=worst,
662	    )
663	    return RegressionReport(
664	        a_run_id=ir_a.manifest.run_id,
665	        b_run_id=ir_b.manifest.run_id,
666	        slug=ir_a.manifest.slug,
667	        alerts=tuple(alerts),
668	        summary=summary,
669	    )
670	
671	
672	def alert_to_dict(alert: RegressionAlert) -> dict[str, Any]:
673	    return {
674	        "severity": alert.severity.value,
675	        "code": alert.code.value,
676	        "message": alert.message,
677	        "a_value": alert.a_value,
678	        "b_value": alert.b_value,
679	        "threshold": alert.threshold,
680	    }
681	
682	
683	def report_to_dict(report: RegressionReport) -> dict[str, Any]:
684	    return {
685	        "a_run_id": report.a_run_id,
686	        "b_run_id": report.b_run_id,
687	        "slug": report.slug,
688	        "alerts": [alert_to_dict(a) for a in report.alerts],
689	        "summary": {
690	            "critical_count": report.summary.critical_count,
691	            "high_count": report.summary.high_count,
692	            "medium_count": report.summary.medium_count,
693	            "info_count": report.summary.info_count,
694	            "worst_severity": report.summary.worst_severity,
695	        },
696	    }

1	"""M-D9 (Phase D): Regression lab — pin + induction diffing.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN M-D9 + Phase D milestone plan: continuous
4	integration of validation-set checks. Every code change runs
5	the validation set; regressions block merge.
6	
7	This module ships **phase 1 — bootstrap**: pin-vs-pin
8	configuration drift + induction-precision drift. Phase 2
9	(BEAT-BOTH dimension scoring) is a separate module.
10	
11	## What it diffs
12	
13	Two `RegressionInputs` snapshots — `baseline` and `current`:
14	  - `pin: ModelPin` — captured via M-D11 phase 1
15	  - `induction_metrics: PrecisionMetrics` — captured via M-D1
16	    benchmark over a validation set
17	  - `manifest: dict | None` — optional pipeline manifest with
18	    abort_status, gate verdicts, etc.
19	
20	For each input pair, computes a `RegressionReport`:
21	  - `pin_drift: tuple[PinDriftField, ...]` — every config field
22	    that changed (per-role models, env vars, retrieval versions,
23	    inductor profile, etc.)
24	  - `induction_drift: tuple[InductionDriftMetric, ...]` — every
25	    induction metric that moved beyond a tolerance (precision,
26	    recall, abstain-precision, abstain-recall, silent-disagreement)
27	  - `manifest_drift: tuple[ManifestDriftField, ...]` — pipeline
28	    verdict / gate flips (abort_status, release_allowed)
29	  - `verdict: GREEN | YELLOW | RED`:
30	    * GREEN: nothing drifted beyond tolerance
31	    * YELLOW: env/inductor drift, but induction precision flat
32	    * RED: induction precision regressed OR pipeline gate flipped
33	
34	CI exit code maps from verdict (RED → non-zero, blocks merge).
35	
36	## Why pin diff is necessary even with content diff
37	
38	Two runs with the same content can have radically different
39	configurations (different model, prompts, gates) that happen to
40	produce the same output on this validation set but diverge
41	elsewhere. Pin diff surfaces silent configuration drift even
42	when content is stable.
43	
44	## Tolerance defaults
45	
46	Numeric metric tolerances are env-overridable per LAW VI.
47	Defaults are conservative-strict in the false-positive direction
48	(better to flag a borderline regression than miss it).
49	"""
50	
51	from __future__ import annotations
52	
53	import os
54	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
55	from enum import Enum
56	from typing import Any
57	
58	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.model_pin import (
59	    ModelPin,
60	    pins_equivalent_for_replay,
61	)
62	from src.polaris_graph.auto_induction.precision_metrics import (
63	    PrecisionMetrics,
64	)
65	
66	
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	# Enumerations
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	
71	
72	class RegressionVerdict(Enum):
73	    """Top-level CI verdict.
74	
75	    GREEN: nothing drifted beyond tolerance — merge OK.
76	    YELLOW: configuration drift but induction precision flat —
77	       operator should review but no hard block.
78	    RED: induction precision regressed OR pipeline gate flipped
79	       — merge blocked. CI exit code non-zero.
80	    """

197	def _precision_tolerance() -> float:
198	    """Max allowed drop in induction precision before flagging
199	    regression. Default: 0.02 (2 percentage points)."""
200	    raw = os.getenv("PG_REGRESSION_PRECISION_TOLERANCE", "0.02")
201	    try:
202	        val = float(raw)
203	    except ValueError as exc:
204	        raise RegressionLabError(
205	            f"PG_REGRESSION_PRECISION_TOLERANCE must be float, got {raw!r}"
206	        ) from exc
207	    if val < 0:
208	        raise RegressionLabError(
209	            f"PG_REGRESSION_PRECISION_TOLERANCE must be >=0, got {val}"
210	        )
211	    return val
212	
213

215	    """Max allowed drop in recall (incl. abstain_recall)."""
216	    raw = os.getenv("PG_REGRESSION_RECALL_TOLERANCE", "0.05")
217	    try:
218	        val = float(raw)
219	    except ValueError as exc:
220	        raise RegressionLabError(
221	            f"PG_REGRESSION_RECALL_TOLERANCE must be float, got {raw!r}"
222	        ) from exc
223	    if val < 0:
224	        raise RegressionLabError(
225	            f"PG_REGRESSION_RECALL_TOLERANCE must be >=0, got {val}"
226	        )
227	    return val

270	
271	def _diff_pin(
272	    baseline: ModelPin, current: ModelPin
273	) -> tuple[PinDriftField, ...]:
274	    """Return every per-field difference between two pins.
275	
276	    Excludes metadata (run_id, captured_at, notes) — those are
277	    expected to differ between runs and aren't config.
278	    """
279	    drifts: list[PinDriftField] = []
280	
281	    if baseline.pin_schema_version != current.pin_schema_version:
282	        drifts.append(
283	            PinDriftField(
284	                field_name="pin_schema_version",
285	                baseline_value=baseline.pin_schema_version,
286	                current_value=current.pin_schema_version,
287	                severity="schema",
288	            )
289	        )
290	
291	    if baseline.llm_models != current.llm_models:
292	        drifts.append(
293	            PinDriftField(
294	                field_name="llm_models",
295	                baseline_value=dict(baseline.llm_models),
296	                current_value=dict(current.llm_models),
297	                severity="config",
298	            )
299	        )
300	
301	    if baseline.llm_providers != current.llm_providers:
302	        drifts.append(
303	            PinDriftField(
304	                field_name="llm_providers",
305	                baseline_value=dict(baseline.llm_providers),
306	                current_value=dict(current.llm_providers),
307	                severity="config",
308	            )
309	        )
310	
311	    if baseline.prompt_version_hashes != current.prompt_version_hashes:
312	        drifts.append(
313	            PinDriftField(
314	                field_name="prompt_version_hashes",
315	                baseline_value=dict(baseline.prompt_version_hashes),
316	                current_value=dict(current.prompt_version_hashes),
317	                severity="config",
318	            )
319	        )
320	
321	    if baseline.retrieval_source_versions != current.retrieval_source_versions:
322	        drifts.append(
323	            PinDriftField(
324	                field_name="retrieval_source_versions",
325	                baseline_value=dict(baseline.retrieval_source_versions),
326	                current_value=dict(current.retrieval_source_versions),
327	                severity="config",
328	            )
329	        )
330	
331	    if baseline.inductor_type != current.inductor_type:
332	        drifts.append(
333	            PinDriftField(
334	                field_name="inductor_type",
335	                baseline_value=baseline.inductor_type,
336	                current_value=current.inductor_type,
337	                severity="config",
338	            )
339	        )
340	
341	    if baseline.inductor_version_hash != current.inductor_version_hash:
342	        drifts.append(
343	            PinDriftField(
344	                field_name="inductor_version_hash",
345	                baseline_value=baseline.inductor_version_hash,
346	                current_value=current.inductor_version_hash,
347	                severity="config",
348	            )
349	        )
350	
351	    if baseline.validation_set_hash != current.validation_set_hash:
352	        # validation_set_hash is the IDENTITY of the benchmark
353	        # dataset. Once it changes, induction precision/recall
354	        # are no longer apples-to-apples — the gate must fail
355	        # closed. Severity "schema" forces a RED verdict in
356	        # diff_regression.
357	        drifts.append(
358	            PinDriftField(
359	                field_name="validation_set_hash",
360	                baseline_value=baseline.validation_set_hash,
361	                current_value=current.validation_set_hash,
362	                severity="schema",
363	            )
364	        )
365	
366	    if baseline.env_snapshot != current.env_snapshot:
367	        # Per-key delta so reviewers don't have to diff two big
368	        # dicts.
369	        all_keys = set(baseline.env_snapshot) | set(current.env_snapshot)
370	        for key in sorted(all_keys):
371	            b_val = baseline.env_snapshot.get(key)
372	            c_val = current.env_snapshot.get(key)
373	            if b_val != c_val:
374	                drifts.append(
375	                    PinDriftField(
376	                        field_name=f"env_snapshot[{key}]",
377	                        baseline_value=b_val,
378	                        current_value=c_val,
379	                        severity="env",
380	                    )
381	                )
382	
383	    return tuple(drifts)
384

386	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
387	# Induction-metric diffing
388	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
389	
390	
391	def _diff_induction(
392	    baseline: PrecisionMetrics, current: PrecisionMetrics
393	) -> tuple[InductionDriftMetric, ...]:
394	    """Return every induction metric that moved beyond tolerance.
395	
396	    Direction-aware: for precision/recall/abstain-precision/
397	    abstain-recall, a DROP is regression. For silent-disagreement
398	    and operator-review-load, a RISE is regression.
399	    """
400	    drifts: list[InductionDriftMetric] = []
401	
402	    prec_tol = _precision_tolerance()
403	    rec_tol = _recall_tolerance()
404	    sd_tol = _silent_disagreement_tolerance()
405	    or_tol = _operator_review_tolerance()
406	
407	    # Precision: drop is regression.
408	    delta = current.precision - baseline.precision
409	    if abs(delta) > prec_tol:
410	        drifts.append(
411	            InductionDriftMetric(
412	                metric=InductionMetric.PRECISION,
413	                baseline_value=baseline.precision,
414	                current_value=current.precision,
415	                delta=delta,
416	                tolerance=prec_tol,
417	                is_regression=delta < -prec_tol,
418	            )
419	        )
420	
421	    # Abstain precision: drop is regression.
422	    delta = current.abstain_precision - baseline.abstain_precision
423	    if abs(delta) > prec_tol:
424	        drifts.append(
425	            InductionDriftMetric(
426	                metric=InductionMetric.ABSTAIN_PRECISION,
427	                baseline_value=baseline.abstain_precision,
428	                current_value=current.abstain_precision,
429	                delta=delta,
430	                tolerance=prec_tol,
431	                is_regression=delta < -prec_tol,
432	            )
433	        )
434	
435	    # Abstain recall: drop is regression.
436	    delta = current.abstain_recall - baseline.abstain_recall
437	    if abs(delta) > rec_tol:
438	        drifts.append(
439	            InductionDriftMetric(
440	                metric=InductionMetric.ABSTAIN_RECALL,
441	                baseline_value=baseline.abstain_recall,
442	                current_value=current.abstain_recall,
443	                delta=delta,
444	                tolerance=rec_tol,
445	                is_regression=delta < -rec_tol,
446	            )
447	        )
448	
449	    # Silent disagreement: rise is regression.
450	    delta = (
451	        current.silent_disagreement_rate - baseline.silent_disagreement_rate
452	    )
453	    if abs(delta) > sd_tol:
454	        drifts.append(
455	            InductionDriftMetric(
456	                metric=InductionMetric.SILENT_DISAGREEMENT_RATE,
457	                baseline_value=baseline.silent_disagreement_rate,
458	                current_value=current.silent_disagreement_rate,
459	                delta=delta,
460	                tolerance=sd_tol,
461	                is_regression=delta > sd_tol,
462	            )
463	        )
464	
465	    # Operator review load: rise is regression (worse UX).
466	    delta = current.operator_review_load - baseline.operator_review_load
467	    if abs(delta) > or_tol:
468	        drifts.append(
469	            InductionDriftMetric(
470	                metric=InductionMetric.OPERATOR_REVIEW_LOAD,
471	                baseline_value=baseline.operator_review_load,
472	                current_value=current.operator_review_load,
473	                delta=delta,
474	                tolerance=or_tol,
475	                is_regression=delta > or_tol,
476	            )
477	        )
478	
479	    return tuple(drifts)
480

482	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
483	# Manifest diffing
484	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
485	
486	
487	def _diff_manifest(
488	    baseline: dict[str, Any] | None, current: dict[str, Any] | None
489	) -> tuple[ManifestDriftField, ...]:
490	    """Return every pipeline-verdict field that flipped.
491	
492	    Reads the LIVE manifest schema produced by
493	    `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` (top-level `status`,
494	    `release_allowed`, nested `adequacy.decision` and
495	    `generator.sentences_verified`).
496	
497	    If either input is None, returns empty tuple (manifest check
498	    skipped — the caller didn't supply pipeline output).
499	    """
500	    if baseline is None or current is None:
501	        return ()
502	
503	    drifts: list[ManifestDriftField] = []
504	
505	    # `status` is the unified pipeline taxonomy:
506	    # success / partial_* / abort_* / error_*. Regression =
507	    # going from "success" to anything else, OR going from any
508	    # partial_* to any abort_*/error_* (degradation within the
509	    # taxonomy).
510	    b_status = baseline.get("status")
511	    c_status = current.get("status")
512	    if b_status != c_status:
513	        is_regression = _status_is_regression(b_status, c_status)
514	        drifts.append(
515	            ManifestDriftField(
516	                field=ManifestDrift.STATUS,
517	                baseline_value=b_status,
518	                current_value=c_status,
519	                is_regression=is_regression,
520	            )
521	        )
522	
523	    # release_allowed is the evaluator-gate verdict.
524	    b_release = baseline.get("release_allowed")
525	    c_release = current.get("release_allowed")
526	    if b_release != c_release:
527	        # Regression if went from True to False.
528	        is_regression = b_release is True and c_release is not True
529	        drifts.append(
530	            ManifestDriftField(
531	                field=ManifestDrift.RELEASE_ALLOWED,
532	                baseline_value=b_release,
533	                current_value=c_release,
534	                is_regression=is_regression,
535	            )
536	        )
537	
538	    # adequacy.decision: "proceed" / "expand" / "abort".
539	    # Regression = proceed -> expand or proceed -> abort, OR
540	    # expand -> abort.
541	    b_adequacy = _nested_get(baseline, "adequacy", "decision")
542	    c_adequacy = _nested_get(current, "adequacy", "decision")
543	    if b_adequacy != c_adequacy:
544	        is_regression = _adequacy_is_regression(b_adequacy, c_adequacy)
545	        drifts.append(
546	            ManifestDriftField(
547	                field=ManifestDrift.ADEQUACY_DECISION,
548	                baseline_value=b_adequacy,
549	                current_value=c_adequacy,
550	                is_regression=is_regression,
551	            )
552	        )
553	
554	    # generator.sentences_verified dropping to zero is a hard
555	    # regression even if status didn't flip.
556	    b_verified = _nested_get(baseline, "generator", "sentences_verified")
557	    c_verified = _nested_get(current, "generator", "sentences_verified")
558	    if (
559	        isinstance(b_verified, int)
560	        and isinstance(c_verified, int)
561	        and b_verified > 0
562	        and c_verified == 0
563	    ):
564	        drifts.append(
565	            ManifestDriftField(
566	                field=ManifestDrift.SENTENCES_VERIFIED_DROPPED_TO_ZERO,
567	                baseline_value=b_verified,
568	                current_value=c_verified,
569	                is_regression=True,
570	            )
571	        )
572	
573	    return tuple(drifts)
574

661	    # runner.UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES (closes the last status-schema-parity drift entry).
662	    "abort_required_entity_ledger_failed": 2,
663	    # I-deepfix-001 U5 (#1344): the U5 synthesis-fires canary aborted a would-be-success run because
664	    # consolidation produced multi-source baskets but composition rendered ZERO multi-cited sentences
665	    # (a pure span-dump regression) — no trustworthy report (tier 2). MUST stay mirrored with
666	    # runner.UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES (status-schema-parity gate).
667	    "abort_synthesis_did_not_fire": 2,
668	    # error — unhandled exception
669	    "error_unexpected": 3,
670	    # I-ready-017 (#1134): journal_only fail-closed no-leak backstop — a
671	    # non-journal row reached the generator (should never happen). Tier 3.
672	    "error_journal_only_leak": 3,
673	    # I-ready-017 FX-06b (#1121): corpus-approval gate vs adequacy artifact
674	    # would score different populations (total_sources or tier_counts diverge);
675	    # defensive fail-loud — no trustworthy report. Tier 3.
676	    "error_corpus_population_mismatch": 3,
677	}
678	
679	# Public alias of the keys for taxonomy-drift testing.
680	KNOWN_STATUS_VALUES: frozenset[str] = frozenset(_STATUS_TIERS)
681	
682	
683	def _status_tier(status: object) -> int:
684	    """Return tier index for a known status, or -1 for unknown.
685	
686	    Unknown values include typos, future taxonomy additions
687	    that haven't been mirrored here, and non-string inputs.
688	    They make `_status_is_regression` return True (fail
689	    closed) — better to flag a borderline case than miss a
690	    real regression.
691	    """
692	    if not isinstance(status, str):
693	        return -1
694	    return _STATUS_TIERS.get(status, -1)
695	
696	
697	def _status_is_regression(
698	    baseline: object, current: object
699	) -> bool:
700	    """A status flip is regression if current is in a worse tier
701	    than baseline. Within-tier flips (e.g.
702	    partial_thin_corpus -> partial_outline_fallback) are NOT
703	    regressions for the bootstrap gate."""
704	    b_tier = _status_tier(baseline)
705	    c_tier = _status_tier(current)
706	    if b_tier < 0 or c_tier < 0:
707	        # Unknown taxonomy values fail closed.
708	        return True
709	    return c_tier > b_tier
710	
711	
712	def _adequacy_is_regression(
713	    baseline: object, current: object
714	) -> bool:
715	    """Adequacy ordering: proceed (best) < expand < abort (worst)."""
716	    order = {"proceed": 0, "expand": 1, "abort": 2}
717	    if baseline not in order or current not in order:
718	        return True  # unknown values fail closed
719	    return order[current] > order[baseline]  # type: ignore[index]
720	
721	
722	def _nested_get(d: dict[str, Any] | None, *keys: str) -> Any:
723	    """Walk nested dict by key path; return None if any link
724	    is missing or non-dict."""
725	    cur: Any = d
726	    for k in keys:
727	        if not isinstance(cur, dict):
728	            return None
729	        cur = cur.get(k)
730	    return cur
731	
732	
733	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
734	# Top-level diff
735	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
736	
737	
738	def diff_regression(
739	    baseline: RegressionInputs, current: RegressionInputs
740	) -> RegressionReport:
741	    """Compute the full regression report.
742	
743	    Verdict logic:
744	      RED if any induction metric is_regression=True OR any
745	        manifest field is_regression=True OR pin_schema_version
746	        differs.
747	      YELLOW if there's any pin/env/manifest drift but no
748	        regression flagged.
749	      GREEN otherwise.
750	    """
751	    pin_drift = _diff_pin(baseline.pin, current.pin)
752	    induction_drift = _diff_induction(
753	        baseline.induction_metrics, current.induction_metrics
754	    )
755	    manifest_drift = _diff_manifest(baseline.manifest, current.manifest)
756	
757	    induction_regression = any(d.is_regression for d in induction_drift)
758	    manifest_regression = any(d.is_regression for d in manifest_drift)
759	    schema_regression = any(
760	        d.severity == "schema" for d in pin_drift
761	    )
762	
763	    if induction_regression or manifest_regression or schema_regression:
764	        verdict = RegressionVerdict.RED
765	    elif pin_drift or induction_drift or manifest_drift:
766	        verdict = RegressionVerdict.YELLOW
767	    else:
768	        verdict = RegressionVerdict.GREEN
769	
770	    return RegressionReport(
771	        verdict=verdict,
772	        pin_drift=pin_drift,
773	        induction_drift=induction_drift,
774	        manifest_drift=manifest_drift,
775	    )
776	
777	
778	def report_to_exit_code(report: RegressionReport) -> int:
779	    """Map a verdict to a CI exit code.
780	
781	    GREEN → 0 (merge OK)
782	    YELLOW → 0 (configuration drift but no regression — operator
783	       review only, doesn't block CI)
784	    RED → 1 (regression detected — block merge)
785	    """
786	    if report.verdict is RegressionVerdict.RED:
787	        return 1
788	    return 0
789	
790	
791	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
792	# Convenience: replay-equivalence shortcut
793	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
794	
795	
796	def pins_replay_equivalent(
797	    baseline: ModelPin, current: ModelPin
798	) -> bool:
799	    """Wrapper around `pins_equivalent_for_replay` for callers
800	    that import only this module."""
801	    return pins_equivalent_for_replay(baseline, current)
802
